intro: Achyrophorus valdesii, also known as Valdes' achyrophorus or Valdivian achyrophorus, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Apiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and infections, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Achyrophorus valdesii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain.
